examples.text: ثُمَّ رَحَلْنَا وَنَزَلْنَا قَرْيَةً تُعْرَفُ بِالْمُوَيْلِحَةِ. ثُمَّ رَحَلْنَا مِنْهَا وَنَزَلْنَا جَزِيرَةَ ٱبْنِ عُمَرَ، وَهِيَ مَدِينَةٌ كَبِيرَةٌ حَسَنَةٌ، مُحِيطٌ بِهَا الْوَادِي، وَلِذٰلِكَ سُمِّيَتْ جَزِيرَةً، وَأَكْثَرُهَا خَرَابٌ.
examples.english: Journeying on, we halted at a village called al-Muwailiha, and from there alighted at Jazirat Ibn ‘Omar, a large and beautiful city surrounded by the river [Tigris], which is the reason why it is called Jazira [‘island’]. The greater part of it is in ruins.

senses.gloss: to make depart; to send out, to dispatch, to urge
to make depart; to transport, to deport, to relocate, to carry over
to make depart; to carry over, to convey

etymology.text:
Form I verb from the root ر ح ل (r ḥ l).

Form II verb from the root ر ح ل (r ḥ l).
